topic: Which models are sensitive vs. insensitive to scale
scope: The model-family decision lens: k-NN/SVM vs. trees.
key_concepts: Not all machine learning algorithms care about scaling and feature distributions.
Distance-based and gradient-based models (k-NN, SVM, neural networks, linear regression with regularization) are highly sensitive to unscaled data.
Tree-based models (Decision Trees, Random Forest, Gradient Boosting) rely completely on value splitting, making them strictly immune and insensitive to magnitude or outlier skewness.
examples: A decision tree cutting 'Age < 30' splits exactly the same whether age is stated as 25 or scaled to 0.2.

relations.builds_toward: The recurring model-family callout across the book.
